calendar.matters:
First And Final Account And Report Of Administrator And Petition For Order
Off Calendar Nov-20-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Darren Ing for Asian Pacific Islander Legal Outreach (API).

The Court granted the petition (subject to an amended order).

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Nuque. Matter Not Reported.

First And Final Account And Report Of Administrator And Petition For Order
Off Calendar Oct-06-2017 Continued To Oct-18-17 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 204

First And Final Account And Report Of Administrator And Petition For Order
Off Calendar Jul-07-2017 Continued To Oct-06-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Theresa Chiong for Dean Ito Taylor (Administrator).

Ms. Choing stated that Carol was unable to move the property into the trust before she passed away. The Court stated that Carol's will disinherits her husband and question why 259 is needed for the separate property. Furthermore the Court questioned if family code 2601 in regards to the community property can be used in terms of estate assets. In response to the question of clear and convincing evidence Ms. Chiong argued that she submitted the domestic violence restraining order request.

The Court questioned where the husband is. Ms. Chiong reported that she spoke to a neighbor and they informed her that he may have passed away and that she did not look into any record of death. The Courte cited California rule of Court 7.52 in regards to notice.

The Court stated that it would case law regarding evidence code 1370, that an explanation of how family law code 2601 can apply to probate estate and a separate memo of points and authority may be filed. Furthermore the Court would like to know how 259 can relate to community property, the Court questioned what efforts were made to locate the step son and a supplement declaration is needed to explain the attempts and efforts made to locate him. The Court granted the side prayer action (Ms. Chiong to prepare a form of order) and informed Ms. Chiong that a partial distribution could be made (a motion is required) and the community property may be held in reserve. The Court continued the first and final account and report of administrator to October 6, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

* Outside the presence of counsel, the Court admits into evidence the following document

9:57am The following Cross-Complainant's exhibit are pre-marked for identification and admitted:

No. Identified Admitted Description
6 07/07/2017 07/07/2017 Unfiled proof of service in FDV-12-809717

Judge John K. Stewart, Clerk: N. Wong, Bailiff: Deputy Neves. Not Reported, D501.

First And Final Account And Report Of Administrator And Petition For Order
Off Calendar Jun-23-2017 Continued To Jul-07-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

First And Final Account And Report Of Administrator And Petition For Order
Off Calendar Jun-05-2017 Continued To Jun-23-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Theresa Chiong for Dean Ito Taylor (Administrator).

The Court questions whether Mr. Harrison has to be deemed deceased and informed Counsel that she needs clear and convincing evidence of the elements in Probate Code 259. Ms. Chiong stated that a hearing was set on the restraining order, that Ms. Davis passed away two weeks before the hearing due to the abuse from Mr. Harrison, and that the temporary restraining order and the police report were submitted as exhibits in the accounting that was filed on September 26, 2016. The First and Final Account and Report of Administrator is continued to June 23, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for an evidentiary hearing. The Court informed Counsel that she will have to file a request for judicial notice.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Mar-21-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Akiko Takeshita for Dean Taylor.

The Court inquired about an appearance by the decedent's estranged spouse. Ms. Takeshita stated that she's had no contact with the decedent's estranged spouse, that she only had contact with his attorney, and that his attorney hasn't heard from him. In addition, Ms. Takeshita stated that she was given a phone number for the decedent's estranged spouse but it doesn't allow you to leave any messages. The Court granted the petition (subject to review of the order). No bond is required.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)
